enonce.texte: Match the following social issues vocabulary with their correct definitions. This exercise reviews key terms from Chapter 1: Civic Responsibilities and Social Issues.

questions.texte: Match each term (a-e) with its correct definition (i-v).

sousQuestions.texte: Female mutilation
sousQuestions.reponse: The practice of cutting or altering female genitalia for non-medical reasons.

sousQuestions.texte: Perpetrators
sousQuestions.reponse: People who commit or carry out harmful acts.

sousQuestions.texte: Abuse of rights
sousQuestions.reponse: The violation or misuse of legal or human rights.

sousQuestions.texte: Violence
sousQuestions.reponse: Behavior involving physical force intended to hurt or kill someone.

sousQuestions.texte: Awareness
sousQuestions.reponse: Knowledge or understanding of a particular issue or situation.

questions.texte: Fill in the blanks with appropriate vocabulary words from the list.

sousQuestions.texte: The government launched a campaign to raise _______ about gender-based violence.
sousQuestions.reponse: awareness

sousQuestions.texte: _______ are often punished severely by the law.
sousQuestions.reponse: Perpetrators

enonce.texte:
Complete the following sentences with the correct health or environment vocabulary words from the box below.

Word Bank: vaccine, hygiene, recycle, erosion, prevention, symptoms, waste, treatment

questions.texte: Choose the correct word to complete each sentence.

sousQuestions.texte: The _______ for this disease includes fever and headache.
sousQuestions.reponse: symptoms

sousQuestions.texte: Good _______ practices can prevent the spread of diseases.
sousQuestions.reponse: hygiene

sousQuestions.texte: The government is promoting _______ to reduce plastic pollution.
sousQuestions.reponse: recycle

sousQuestions.texte: Soil _______ is a major environmental problem in many regions.
sousQuestions.reponse: erosion

sousQuestions.texte: The _______ is more effective than cure.
sousQuestions.reponse: prevention

enonce.texte: Rewrite the following sentences in the passive voice. This exercise reviews the passive voice from Lesson 3: Discussing the Educational System.

questions.texte: Rewrite the following active sentences in the passive voice.

sousQuestions.texte: The government launched a health campaign last year.
sousQuestions.reponse: A health campaign was launched by the government last year.

sousQuestions.texte: Students are organizing a recycling program at school.
sousQuestions.reponse: A recycling program is being organized by students at school.

sousQuestions.texte: The committee will review the applications next week.
sousQuestions.reponse: The applications will be reviewed by the committee next week.

sousQuestions.texte: The teacher explained the lesson clearly.
sousQuestions.reponse: The lesson was explained clearly by the teacher.

enonce.texte: Convert the following direct speeches into reported speech. This exercise reviews reported speech from Lesson 13: Following Instructions.

questions.texte: Change the following direct speech into reported speech.

sousQuestions.texte: "We will start the project tomorrow," said the manager.
sousQuestions.reponse: The manager said that they would start the project the next day.

sousQuestions.texte: "I am going to apply for the job," she told me.
sousQuestions.reponse: She told me that she was going to apply for the job.

sousQuestions.texte: "You should recycle your waste," the mayor urged.
sousQuestions.reponse: The mayor urged that we should recycle our waste.

sousQuestions.texte: "I have already submitted my application," he said.
sousQuestions.reponse: He said that he had already submitted his application.

enonce.texte: Complete the following conditional sentences (Type 1) with the correct verb forms. This exercise reviews conditionals from Lesson 8: Understanding Health Campaigns.

questions.texte: Complete the conditional sentences with the correct form of the verbs in parentheses.

sousQuestions.texte: If you _______ (recycle) more, you _______ (help) the environment.
sousQuestions.reponse: recycle, will help

sousQuestions.texte: If she _______ (study) hard, she _______ (pass) the exam.
sousQuestions.reponse: studies, will pass

sousQuestions.texte: If we _______ (not reduce) waste, pollution _______ (increase).
sousQuestions.reponse: don't reduce, will increase

sousQuestions.texte: If you _______ (take) the vaccine, you _______ (be) protected.
sousQuestions.reponse: take, will be

enonce.texte: Complete the following sentences with the correct modal verbs. This exercise reviews modals for advice, obligation, and speculation.

questions.texte: Choose the correct modal verb to complete each sentence.

sousQuestions.texte: You _______ submit your application before the deadline. (obligation)
sousQuestions.reponse: have to / must

sousQuestions.texte: You _______ wear a mask in crowded places. (advice)
sousQuestions.reponse: should

sousQuestions.texte: He _______ be at the office now, but I'm not sure. (speculation)
sousQuestions.reponse: may / might / could

sousQuestions.texte: You _______ eat too much sugar. (negative advice)
sousQuestions.reponse: shouldn't

questions.texte: Rewrite the following sentences using the appropriate modal verb.

sousQuestions.texte: It is necessary for you to get a visa before traveling.
sousQuestions.reponse: You must get a visa before traveling.

sousQuestions.texte: Perhaps she is at the job interview right now.
sousQuestions.reponse: She may be at the job interview right now.

enonce.texte:
Read the following text carefully and answer the questions below. This exercise tests your reading comprehension skills.

Environmental Challenges in Cameroon

Cameroon faces several environmental challenges that require urgent attention. Deforestation is a major concern, as the country loses thousands of hectares of forest each year due to illegal logging and agricultural expansion. This has led to soil erosion and loss of biodiversity. Additionally, waste management in urban areas like Douala and Yaoundé has become a serious problem. Many cities struggle to collect and dispose of garbage properly, leading to pollution and health risks.

To address these issues, various initiatives have been launched. Recycling programs have been introduced in some communities, encouraging citizens to separate their waste and reduce pollution. The government has also implemented laws to protect forests and promote sustainable agriculture. However, more efforts are needed to raise public awareness and ensure effective implementation of these measures.

questions.texte: Answer the following questions based on the text.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify the main environmental problems mentioned in Cameroon.
sousQuestions.reponse: Deforestation, soil erosion, loss of biodiversity, and waste management problems in urban areas.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain the causes of deforestation in Cameroon according to the text.
sousQuestions.reponse: Illegal logging and agricultural expansion.

sousQuestions.texte: What solutions have been implemented to address these environmental challenges?
sousQuestions.reponse: Recycling programs have been introduced, and the government has implemented laws to protect forests and promote sustainable agriculture.

sousQuestions.texte: Analyze why more efforts are needed despite the existing initiatives.
sousQuestions.reponse: More efforts are needed to raise public awareness and ensure effective implementation of environmental protection measures.

enonce.texte: Complete the following exercises that review various grammar points covered during the year including passive voice, reported speech, conditionals, and modals.

questions.texte: Rewrite the following sentences as indicated in parentheses.

sousQuestions.texte: The government will launch a new health campaign. (passive voice)
sousQuestions.reponse: A new health campaign will be launched by the government.

sousQuestions.texte: "We have already completed the project," she said. (reported speech)
sousQuestions.reponse: She said that they had already completed the project.

sousQuestions.texte: If you don't practice regularly, you won't improve your skills. (make the conditional sentence positive)
sousQuestions.reponse: If you practice regularly, you will improve your skills.

sousQuestions.texte: It is necessary for you to bring your identification card. (use the appropriate modal)
sousQuestions.reponse: You must bring your identification card.

enonce.texte: Read the following text and fill in the blanks with appropriate vocabulary words from the themes studied: social issues, health, environment, technology, and work.

questions.texte: Fill in the blanks with the correct vocabulary words.

sousQuestions.texte: The _______ of women's rights is a serious social issue that needs to be addressed.
sousQuestions.reponse: abuse / violation

sousQuestions.texte: Good _______ practices include washing your hands regularly and covering your mouth when you cough.
sousQuestions.reponse: hygiene

sousQuestions.texte: Many companies now use _______ media to promote their products and services.
sousQuestions.reponse: social

sousQuestions.texte: To apply for a job, you need to submit your CV and a _______ letter.
sousQuestions.reponse: cover

sousQuestions.texte: Soil _______ can be prevented by planting trees and building windbreaks.
sousQuestions.reponse: erosion

enonce.texte: Write a persuasive letter to a friend explaining why they should consider a career in a field that contributes to society. Use vocabulary from Lesson 14 and appropriate future tenses.

questions.texte: Write a letter of about 250 words persuading your friend to choose a career that helps others.
questions.reponse: A well-structured persuasive letter that includes: introduction, reasons for choosing a helping profession, explanation of career opportunities, future plans using will and be going to, persuasive arguments, and conclusion.

sousQuestions.texte: Introduction: Greet your friend and introduce the topic
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: Dear [Friend], I'm writing to share my thoughts about career choices and why I believe you should consider...

sousQuestions.texte: Reasons: At least 3 reasons why a helping profession is valuable
sousQuestions.reponse: Examples: You will make a difference in people's lives, the work is meaningful, you will contribute to society's development.

sousQuestions.texte: Future plans: Use will and be going to correctly
sousQuestions.reponse: Examples: I am going to study medicine because... I will work hard to...

sousQuestions.texte: Conclusion: Persuade and encourage your friend
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: I believe you will find great satisfaction in helping others. I hope you will consider this path.

enonce.texte: Transform the following paragraph by applying different grammar rules: change passive to active voice, convert direct speech to reported speech, and rewrite conditional sentences.

questions.texte: Transform the following paragraph as instructed.

sousQuestions.texte: Original: "The recycling program was launched by the city council. The mayor said, 'We will reduce waste by 50% this year.' If people participate, the program will be successful."
sousQuestions.reponse: Transformed: The city council launched the recycling program. The mayor said that they would reduce waste by 50% that year. If people participate, the program will be successful.

sousQuestions.texte: Original: "The report on climate change was published by the environmental agency. The director said, 'We must act now to protect our planet.' If we don't act, the consequences will be severe."
sousQuestions.reponse: Transformed: The environmental agency published the report on climate change. The director said that they must act then to protect their planet. If we don't act, the consequences will be severe.
